title: Enclosures for facilitating activities in space, and associated systems and methods
abstract: Enclosures for facilitating activities in space, and associated systems and methods, are disclosed. A representative system includes a spacecraft having an enclosed interior volume (which can be formed by an inflatable membrane) and one or more unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) carried by the spacecraft and positioned to deploy into the enclosed interior volume. The system can include a remote-control system to control the one or more UAVs from a terrestrial location while the spacecraft is in space. A wireless charging system can provide electrical power to the one or more UAVs. A representative method includes configuring one or more controllers to launch a first spacecraft to a first orbit, launch a second spacecraft to a second orbit, move the first spacecraft to the second orbit, dock the first spacecraft with the second spacecraft, and broadcast an event within an interior volume of the first spacecraft to a terrestrial location.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS I/We claim: 1 . A space system for providing entertainment, the system comprising: a spacecraft having an enclosed interior volume; and one or more unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) carried by the spacecraft and positioned to deploy into the enclosed interior volume, 2. The system of claim 1 wherein the spacecraft comprises one or more thrusters positioned to cause the spacecraft to rotate about an axis to create centripetal force. 3. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a remote-control system coupieable to the one or more UAVs to control the one or more UAVs from a terrestrial location while the spacecraft is in space. 4. The system of claim 1 wherein the spacecraft is an upper stage portion coupieable to a lower stage portion for launch. 5. The system of claim 1 wherein the spacecraft comprises an inflatable membrane, the inflatable membrane containing the enclosed interior volume. 6. The system of claim 5 wherein the inflatable membrane comprises one or more transparent portions and/or translucent portions. 7. The system of claim 6 wherein a majority of the inflatable membrane comprises transparent portions and/or translucent portions. 8. The system of claim 1 , wherein the one or more UAVs comprises a first UAV and a second UAV, wherein the first UAV is configured to initiate a reduced-functionality 

 mode of the second UAV via physical contact between the first UAV and the second UAV or a signal transmitted by the first UAV, 9. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a wireless charging system to provide electrical power to the one or more UAVs. 10. The system of claim 9 wherein the enclosed interior volume comprises a surface positioned to reflect radio-frequency signals to facilitate transmission of wireless charging signals emitted by the wireless charging system. 1 1 . The system of claim 10 wherein neither a length, nor a width, nor a radius of the volume is a unit-multiple of a quarter-wavelength of the radio-frequency signals. 12. The system of claim 9 wherein the spacecraft comprises a Faraday cage surrounding the enclosed interior volume. 13. The system of claim 1 , further comprising a display positioned within the enclosed interior volume, the display being configured to show one or more values associated with a contest involving the one or more UAVs. 14. The system of claim 1 , wherein the spacecraft is a first spacecraft, the system further comprising one or more controllers configured to implement a routine that includes: moving the first spacecraft from a first orbit to a second orbit, the first orbit being larger than the second orbit; docking the first spacecraft with a second spacecraft; and returning the first spacecraft to the first orbit. 15. The system of claim 14 wherein the one or more controllers are further configured to implement a routine that includes facilitating remote control of the UAVs from a terrestrial location. 16. The system of claim 1 , further comprising one or more holders configured to transport the one or more UAVs to space and to deploy the one or more UAVs when the spacecraft is in space, 17. A spacecraft system, comprising: a flexible membrane configured to deploy from a spacecraft to enclose an interior volume filled with one or more gases, wherein the flexible membrane is generally transparent or generally translucent; and a plurality of cameras positioned to acquire visual images of the interior volume. 18. The spacecraft system of claim 17, further comprising a stage or a playing surface configured to facilitate entertainment activities including sports or concerts. 19. The spacecraft system of claim 18, comprising a playing surface including artificial turf. 20. The spacecraft system of claim 17, further comprising one or more thrusters positioned to cause the spacecraft to rotate about an axis to create centripetal force relative to the flexible membrane. 21 . The spacecraft system of claim 17, further comprising a receptacle configured to contain and deploy an unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV). 22. The spacecraft system of claim 21 , further comprising a wireless charging system configured to provide wireless electrical power to the UAV. 23. The spacecraft system of claim 17, further comprising one or more flexible bulkheads configured to separate the interior volume into a plurality of interior volumes, 24. The spacecraft system of claim 17, further comprising an indicator configured to communicate to a viewer an orientation of the spacecraft, wherein the indicator is configured to be modified from a terrestrial location. 25. The spacecraft of claim 17, further comprising an indicator configured to communicate to a viewer an orientation of the spacecraft, and a controller programmed with instructions that, when executed, modify the indicator. 26. The spacecraft system of claim 17, further comprising one or more controllers configured to implement a routine that includes: moving the spacecraft from a first orbit to a second orbit, the first orbit being larger than the second orbit; docking the spacecraft with a capsule; broadcasting, using the plurality of cameras, an event within the interior volume; and returning the spacecraft to the first orbit. 27. The spacecraft system of claim 17, further comprising one or more terrestrial facilities comprising displays and remote controls configured to control one or more devices in the interior volume. 28. A spacecraft comprising: an inflatable membrane filled with one or more gases and forming an enclosed interior volume; a surface positioned within the enclosed interior volume, the surface comprising an indicator representing a top, bottom, or side orientation to provide an orientation reference to a viewer of the indicator. 29. The spacecraft of claim 28 wherein the surface is an interior wall of the enclosed interior volume and the indicator comprises a color at least partially covering the interior wall, 30. The spacecraft of claim 28, further comprising one or more controllers configured to implement a routine that includes changing a position or color of the indicator. 31 . The spacecraft of claim 28 wherein at least a portion of the membrane is transparent or translucent, 32. The spacecraft of claim 28, further comprising one or more thrusters positioned to cause the spacecraft to rotate about one or more axes. 33. The spacecraft of claim 32 wherein the indicator represents an orientation aligned with a simulated gravitational force provided by rotating the spacecraft using the one or more thrusters. 34. The spacecraft of claim 28, further comprising a plurality of cameras configured to acquire and transmit visual images of the enclosed interior volume, and one or more controllers configured to implement a routine that includes: moving the spacecraft from a first orbit to a second orbit; docking the spacecraft with another spacecraft; broadcasting, using the plurality of cameras, an event within the interior volume; and returning the spacecraft to the first orbit. 35. A method of operating a space system, the method comprising configuring one or more controllers to: launch a first spacecraft to a first orbit; launch a second spacecraft to a second orbit; move the first spacecraft to the second orbit; dock the first spacecraft with the second spacecraft; and 

 broadcast to a terrestrial location, using a plurality of cameras, an event within an interior volume of the first spacecraft. 36. The method of claim 35 wherein the event is a concert. 37. The method of claim 35 wherein the event is a sports competition. 38. The method of claim 35, further comprising configuring the one or more controllers to deploy a flexible membrane to form the interior volume of the first spacecraft. 39. The method of claim 35, further comprising configuring the one or more controllers to deploy one or more unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) within the interior volume, 40. The method of claim 35, further comprising configuring the one or more controllers to rotate the first spacecraft about one or more axes when the first spacecraft is in the second orbit.
